title: Fresh Start Housing Scheme
text:
Fresh Start Housing Scheme - SupportGoWhere
Fresh Start Housing Scheme
Share link
Helps families with children who are currently living in public rental flats to own a 2-room Flexi flat or 3-room flat on a shorter lease.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Enhanced CPF Housing Grant of up to $120,000 for eligible first-timer families
Fresh Start Housing Grant of $75,000 for eligible second-timer families
Resale levy cap of $30,000 for eligible second-timer families
HDB Concessionary Loan is subject to credit assessment
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

Both first-timer and second-timer families

Purchase a 2-room Flexi or 3-room Standard flat on a shorter lease in HDB sales launches i.e. Build-To-Order (BTO) or Sale of Balance Flats (SBF) exercises, or open booking of flats

HDB Concessionary Loan is subject to credit assessment




First-timer families

Enhanced CPF Housing Grant of up to $120,000 




Second-timer families

Fresh Start Housing Grant of $75,000 ($60,000 will be disbursed into CPF Ordinary Accounts before key collection; $15,000 may be disbursed after key collection into CPF Ordinary Accounts over 5 years)

Resale levy cap of $30,000

Who is this for?

Have at least 1 parent and 1 child who are Singapore Citizens

Applicant and spouse (if applicable) must be aged at least 35 and below 55 years old

Child must be aged below 21

Household monthly income of $7,000 and below

Stayed in public rental flat for at least 1 year

Have occupied public rental flat for at least 1 year, without accumulating 3 or more months of rental arrears in the preceding 12 months

Have not received any public rental tenancy discount under Relocation, Sale of Flat to Sitting Tenants, or Rent & Purchase Schemes

Do not own other properties overseas or locally, and have not disposed of any within the last 30 months

How to apply?

You can apply online at the HDB website.

Where can I find help?

View more details

HDB Branch Service Line: 6225-5432

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 22 May 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
Check now
